imageDescription: Two children with hands moving together at chest height, illustrating a combining/joining gesture
notes: Promoted from hold to approved: image shows a clear two-person gesture consistent with combining/adding quantities together.

imageDescription: Two children with hands moving downward/apart with arrows, illustrating a separating/taking-away gesture
notes: Promoted from hold to approved: image shows hands separating/moving apart, consistent with a subtraction gesture.

imageDescription: Close-up of a hand with fingers spread, with a small arrow indicating repeated/multiplying motion
notes: Promoted from hold to approved: hand gesture with repetition arrow is consistent with a multiplication concept sign.

imageDescription: A boy in profile clapping/separating his hands with a small directional arrow, illustrating a splitting motion

newGlossaryTerm.english: division / to divide / to share out

notes: Promoted from hold to approved: hands splitting apart gesture is consistent with division/sharing concept.

imageDescription: Two children holding both palms up flat and level, with downward arrows, illustrating an 'equal/balanced' gesture
notes: Promoted from hold to approved: two flat level palms is a plausible 'equals/balance' gesture.

imageDescription: Two children with thumbs-up hands and upward arrows, illustrating a 'bigger/more' comparison gesture
notes: Promoted from hold to approved: thumbs-up with upward arrows is consistent with a 'greater than/bigger' comparison gesture.

imageDescription: Caption 'butungi' visible above two children; one holds a thumbs-up hand pointed downward/sideways with arrows, illustrating a 'smaller/less' comparison gesture
notes: Promoted from hold to approved: gesture pairs with the 'Greater than' image as the smaller/lesser comparison. 'Matidi' (small) already exists in glossary as the natural antonym pairing.

imageDescription: A boy in profile pushing both hands forward/together at chest height, illustrating a combining/totaling gesture
notes: Already approved; image (hands gathering together) is consistent with 'Total'. Kept as is.

imageDescription: Two children, one with both hands together at chest forming a shape, the other with hands apart, an arrow visible at upper right -- illustrating a 'splitting into parts' gesture
notes: Promoted from hold to approved: two-handed splitting gesture is consistent with a 'fraction/part of a whole' concept. Acholi translation is a best-effort descriptive phrase (literally 'one of many') for this abstract math term.

imageDescription: A forearm with a hand marking/pointing to the midpoint of the arm with a double-headed arrow, illustrating dividing something into two equal parts
notes: Image shows a gesture marking the midpoint of the forearm, consistent with 'Half'. Kept as is.

imageDescription: A bunch of bananas with the stem visible at top
notes: Originally labeled 'Quarter (1/4)' under math; image is clearly a bunch of bananas, not a fraction concept. Relabeled and moved to food. 'Lapena' (banana, alias 'matooke') already exists in glossary.

imageDescription: A forearm with a hand marking/pointing along the arm with a directional arrow, similar to the 'Half' gesture but with a single-direction arrow
notes: Image shows an arm-marking gesture similar to the 'Half' entry, plausible as a 'portion/percentage' sign. Kept as is; Acholi uses a borrowed/loan term for this modern concept.

imageDescription: A close-up of a hand bringing fingers to the mouth, with a small circled '2' marker -- a hand-to-mouth eating gesture
notes: Originally labeled 'More than (>)' under math; image shows a hand-to-mouth eating gesture (part of a recurring numbered eating-gesture sequence found across pages 13-14), not a comparison symbol. Relabeled to 'Eat' and moved to actions.

imageDescription: A pea pod with several round peas drawn around/inside it
notes: Originally labeled 'Total' under math; image is clearly a pea pod with peas, not a math total. Relabeled and moved to food. Acholi term is a best-effort descriptive phrase; 'nyig' (beans) already exists in glossary as the closest related legume term.

imageDescription: An avocado cut open showing the large round pit/seed inside
notes: Originally labeled 'Decimal Point' under math; image is clearly an avocado with its pit, not a decimal point. Relabeled and moved to food.

imageDescription: A hand sliding along the forearm with a downward arrow and a small circled '1' marker, part of a recurring numbered gesture sequence
notes: Image shows a generic hand-sliding-along-arm gesture with a step marker (1), part of a recurring multi-step diagram seen across pages 12-14. Does not visually suggest 'Infinity', but no confident alternative concrete label could be determined from this single cropped panel. Held for further review.

imageDescription: A cracked egg shell with the broken halves and yolk visible
notes: Originally labeled 'Math Quiz' under math; image is clearly a cracked egg, not a quiz. Relabeled and moved to food. 'Tong' (egg) already exists in glossary.

imageDescription: A plain unmarked oval/egg-like outline shape with no distinguishing internal detail
notes: Originally labeled 'Answer'; image is just a plain, undecorated oval outline with no distinguishing features -- too generic/illegible to confidently identify as any specific vocabulary item (possibly a fragment of the neighboring egg illustration). Rejected as non-concrete reference material.

imageDescription: Two hands with fingers interlocked/crossed together at chest height, with a small circled '1' marker
notes: Originally labeled 'Result' under math; image shows two hands clasping/interlocking fingers, which does not depict a math 'result'. Relabeled to 'Together' (a common two-handed joining gesture) and moved to actions.

imageDescription: Two hands forming a peaked/pointed shape with the index finger of one hand extended upward into the peak
notes: Image shows a distinct two-handed peaked gesture; kept as 'Sum' (plausible math vocabulary gesture, distinct from neighboring 'Difference' image). Acholi is a best-effort descriptive phrase ('add up everything').

imageDescription: Two hands forming a peaked/pointed shape with the index finger extended, similar to but distinguishable from the 'Sum' gesture
notes: Image shows a two-handed peaked gesture similar to but distinguishable from 'Sum'; kept as 'Difference'. Acholi is a best-effort descriptive phrase for this abstract math term.

imageDescription: A round citrus-fruit-like shape with internal segment lines, resembling an orange cut in half or a similar round fruit
notes: Originally labeled 'Quotient' under math; image is a round segmented fruit resembling an orange, not a math quotient. Relabeled and moved to food.

imageDescription: Two hands -- one open, one in a fist -- with a circular motion arrow around them
notes: Image shows a distinct two-handed circular gesture; kept as 'Remainder'. Acholi is a best-effort descriptive phrase ('what remains').

imageDescription: Two hands -- one open, one in a fist -- with a circular motion arrow, nearly identical to the gesture used for 'Remainder'
notes: Originally labeled 'End Math' (a section/meta marker, not a real sign). The image shows a circular two-hand gesture nearly identical to the 'Remainder' image; relabeled to 'Continue' (a concrete action consistent with a circular/repeating motion) and moved to actions. 'Mede' (continue) already exists in the UI glossary.

imageDescription: A boy with one hand raised near his chin/chest showing a single extended finger
notes: Promoted from hold to approved: single-finger gesture near the body is consistent with 'Single/One'. 'Acel' (one) already exists in glossary; 'keken' (only/alone) used to convey 'single'.

imageDescription: Two hands with index fingers extended and a double-headed vertical arrow, illustrating a repeated/doubled motion
notes: Promoted from hold to approved: two-fingered gesture with repeated/double arrow is consistent with 'Double'. Acholi uses reduplication of 'aryo' (two) for emphasis/doubling.

imageDescription: A hand with index finger pointing down onto a fist, with a downward arrow above
notes: Promoted from hold to approved: gesture is part of the same single/double/triple sequence as the two neighboring entries; kept as 'Triple' for consistency. Acholi uses reduplication of 'adek' (three).

imageDescription: A boy with both hands clasped together at chest height, one hand sliding down the forearm with a downward arrow; the word 'friend' is visible printed below the illustration
notes: Originally labeled 'Many/A lot' under quantities; the original page caption 'friend' is visible at the bottom of the image, and the two-handed clasping gesture is consistent with a 'Friend' sign. Relabeled and moved to family.

imageDescription: A boy with one fist near his chest and the other hand sliding along his upper arm/shoulder with an arrow; a partial caption ending in '...unt' is visible below the illustration
notes: Originally labeled 'Few/Some' under quantities; the partial caption '...unt' visible at the bottom strongly suggests 'Aunt' (cut off from a family-vocabulary page). Relabeled and moved to family. Acholi term is a best-effort approximation for this kinship term (exact term should be confirmed by a native speaker).

imageDescription: A boy with one hand near his chin/mouth and the other hand sliding down the forearm with a downward arrow, part of the same family-vocabulary page as the 'Friend' and 'Aunt' images
notes: Image appears to belong to the same family-vocabulary page as the neighboring 'Friend'/'Aunt' entries (similar two-handed sliding gesture starting near the face), suggesting the 'Enough' label may also be wrong. However, no caption text is visible and no confident alternative concrete label (e.g., a specific family member) could be determined. Held for further review.

imageDescription: A close-up fragment of a hand with curled fingers and short motion/tapping lines beneath it
notes: Image is an isolated cropped fragment of a hand with tapping motion lines; no concrete single vocabulary item can be confidently identified, and 'Series' does not match. Rejected as non-concrete reference material.

imageDescription: A hand showing a 'V' shape (index and middle fingers extended) with a side-to-side double arrow above
notes: 'Item 1' is meta/placeholder list wording, not a real vocabulary item. The image is a generic 'V' handshape fragment with a motion arrow; no confident concrete single-word label could be determined. Rejected as non-concrete reference material.

imageDescription: A close-up of a hand bringing fingers to the mouth/chin area, part of the same recurring hand-to-mouth eating gesture sequence seen elsewhere on these pages
notes: Originally labeled 'Item 2' (meta/placeholder list wording) under quantities; image shows the same hand-to-mouth eating gesture as numbers_13_247. Relabeled to 'Eat' and moved to actions, matching 'Camo' already used for that entry.

imageDescription: A forearm and hand with a double-headed horizontal motion arrow near the wrist, an isolated cropped fragment
notes: 'Item 3' is meta/placeholder list wording, not a real vocabulary item. The image is a generic forearm/motion-arrow fragment with no clear referent. Rejected as non-concrete reference material.

imageDescription: A loaf of bread (sliced/rounded shape with a smaller piece beside it)
notes: Originally labeled 'Item 4' (meta/placeholder list wording) under quantities; image is clearly a loaf of bread. Relabeled and moved to food. 'Mugaati' is a widely used East African (Luganda-origin) loanword for bread also common in everyday Acholi speech.

imageDescription: A cup/mug with a handle, containing liquid
notes: Originally labeled 'Item 5' (meta/placeholder list wording) under quantities; image is clearly a cup/mug. Relabeled and moved to food. Acholi term is a best-effort descriptive phrase (literally 'small pot').

imageDescription: A boy with both hands brought up to his mouth and an upward arrow, with a small circled '1' marker -- the same recurring hand-to-mouth eating gesture seen elsewhere on these pages
notes: Originally labeled 'Grouping' under quantities; image shows the same recurring hand-to-mouth eating gesture as numbers_13_247 and numbers_14_271, not a 'grouping' concept. Relabeled to 'Eat' and moved to actions.

imageDescription: A forearm and fist with a vertical double-headed arrow near the wrist, decorated with red nail polish; an isolated cropped fragment
notes: Image is a generic forearm/fist fragment with a shaking motion arrow; cannot confirm it depicts 'Correct/Right', but no confident alternative concrete label could be determined either. Held for further review.

imageDescription: A man holding one hand flat against his forehead/temple, looking forward, with small motion marks near his temple
notes: Image shows a hand-to-forehead gesture (commonly associated with 'thinking'/'looking'), which does not match 'Wrong/Error' (no headshake, frown, or rejection gesture present). Rejected as a mislabeled entry; recommend re-review with a candidate label of 'Think' once confirmed by a native signer, but not confident enough to assert that relabel here.

imageDescription: A woman shown with two labeled hand positions ('1' near the head/ears, '2' with both palms raised facing forward at chest height)
notes: Image shows a two-step hand-position diagram (numbered 1 and 2) that does not obviously match 'Try again'; genuinely ambiguous as to which concept it depicts. Held for further review.

imageDescription: A woman holding both hands in fists at waist height with vertical double-headed shaking arrows on each wrist, pink nail polish visible
notes: Promoted from hold to approved: two-fisted shaking/celebratory gesture is plausibly consistent with an enthusiastic 'Well done' sign. 'Ber tutwal' (excellent/very good) already exists in the UI glossary as the natural Acholi phrase for this.

imageDescription: A cropped fragment of large stylized teal/black lettering, ending in '...ck' -- a text/title graphic, not an illustration
notes: Image is a fragment of decorative page-title text (no person, hands, or object), not a sign illustration at all. Rejected as non-sign reference material.

imageDescription: A cropped fragment of large stylized lettering spelling 'leaf' with additional teal shapes -- a text/title graphic, not an illustration
notes: Image is a fragment of decorative page-title text (no person, hands, or object), not a sign illustration at all. Rejected as non-sign reference material.
